concerns.text: FR-007 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping the definition of 'unethical use' over thresholds {0.5, 0.6, 0.7}. The source idea defines the predictor as 'frequency of AI use for ambiguous tasks' (a continuous or ordinal frequency metric), not a binary classification derived from a threshold. This requirement introduces a new operationalization (binary thresholding) and a specific sensitivity analysis not requested by the idea, constituting over-reach and potential scope drift.

US-003 states the system must 'filter for respondents who report AI usage'. The source idea asks to compare 'individuals who utilize AI tools... than non-users'. Filtering out non-users eliminates the control group required to test the hypothesis (comparing users vs. non-users). The spec must retain non-users to serve as the baseline for the regression analysis, not exclude them.

FR-001 requires filtering for records where the 'AI usage indicator is present'. Similar to US-003, this implies excluding non-users. The research question explicitly requires a comparison between users and non-users; excluding non-users makes the primary regression (comparing groups) impossible. This is an under-reach of the comparative requirement in the idea.
